purpose: Machine-readable boundary for a fresh public-repository export.
notes: lib/scoring/explanations.ts is public-safe: it is a pure string formatter over already-computed Opportunity/ScoreResult fields (reasons, risks, factorNotes), and imports only lib/types and the already-public lib/scoring/covered-score.ts. The ScoreBreakdown factor-name vocabulary it renders is already exposed by covered-score.ts's ScoreInput type, so this file adds no new private surface. No proprietary weighting or tuned decision logic lives here; that stays in scoring-engine/** and the private adapters.

data/mock-opportunities.ts is public-safe: it is static demo fixture data plus formatting calls into lib/scoring/covered-score.ts and lib/scoring/explanations.ts (both public-safe) and lib/providers/player-avatar.ts (generic SVG avatar generator, no private dependency). It no longer has any conditional dependency now that explanations.ts is resolved.

lib/knowledge/live-board.ts never existed in this repository; the real board-orchestration module is lib/providers/live-board.ts. The stale alias entry has been removed from this manifest rather than carried forward.

lib/providers/live-board.ts is private-only: a ~3,200-line orchestration engine that fetches from every provider adapter, calls normalizeSharpApiProps/matchOfficialInjuryRecord-style enrichment, and invokes the private Worker scorer. Splitting a public-safe read-only summary out of it would require a genuine refactor rather than manifest reclassification, so the whole module and its direct dependents are excluded from the public export instead of partially relabeled.

app/(dashboard)/settings/page.tsx and components/providers/odds-api-slate.tsx are excluded from the public export solely because they call lib/providers/live-board.ts (getLiveBoardHealthSnapshot / getLatestBoardRefreshSummary / parseTheOddsApiCache).

app/(dashboard)/sharpapi/page.tsx and components/providers/sharpapi-slate.tsx (the SharpAPI operational/provider-audit UI page) were deleted outright, not manifest-excluded: the page was the only remaining primary-navigation-exposed provider-audit view, removing the nav link alone (an earlier session's fix) left the route still compiled and directly reachable, and the underlying component had zero other importers once the page was gone. Its dependencies (provider-pull-audit.ts, normalize-sharpapi.ts, provider-pull-browser.tsx, sharpapi.ts, db/provider-cache.ts) contained no private imports and remain public-safe/available in their own right -- only the UI page/component was removed. The backend SharpAPI provider adapter (lib/providers/sharpapi.ts), normalization (lib/providers/normalize-sharpapi.ts), and the private ingestion/matching pipeline (lib/knowledge/sharp-ingestion-job.ts, lib/knowledge/sharp-matching.ts, lib/providers/sharpapi-refresh.ts, etc.) are untouched and unaffected by this removal.

lib/providers/provider-registry.ts was reclassified public-safe after a code fix (this repair branch): it previously imported lib/providers/request-policy.ts (private) and rendered exact daily/monthly provider budget numbers via unused `quota`/`cadence`/`notes` fields on ProviderDiagnostic. Those fields were dead code (grepped: never read by any component) and have been removed along with the request-policy import; the module now reports only id/label/status/purpose/sports/browserSafe.

app/api/explanations/route.ts is private-only: its only purpose is wiring the private lib/ai/explanation-adapter.ts (prompt strategy) and the private lib/providers/live-board.ts to an HTTP handler. It has no current UI caller (grepped) and no public-safe alternative implementation.

lib/knowledge/adapters/index.ts is private-only: it imports the private basketball.ts and mlb.ts adapters directly, and its only importer is the private lib/knowledge/scoring-service.ts (grepped, zero other importers).

lib/knowledge/sharp-ingestion.ts, lib/knowledge/sharp-odds-ingestion.ts, and lib/knowledge/enrichment/jobs.ts are private-only: each imports lib/knowledge/matching.ts or lib/knowledge/sharp-matching.ts directly (identity-matching heuristics).
lib/providers/sharpapi-refresh.ts is private-only: it imports lib/knowledge/sharp-ingestion.ts and lib/providers/request-policy.ts, both private.

lib/ops/github-actions-pipeline.ts is private-only: it directly imports lib/knowledge/scoring-service.ts, lib/providers/live-board.ts, and lib/knowledge/sharp-ingestion-job.ts. This is the GitHub Actions pipeline orchestrator; GitHub Actions owns live pipeline orchestration per AGENTS.md and stays in the private repository.

Tests paired with private-only modules remain private unless a later public contract decision explicitly retains them. scripts/public-export.mjs enforces this automatically: excluding a file also excludes its .test.ts/.test.tsx sibling, so paired tests do not need individual manifest entries.

app/(dashboard)/odds-api/page.tsx is private-only: it renders components/providers/odds-api-slate.tsx directly (found by scripts/public-export.mjs's import-closure check, which scans every included file, not just the manifest's publicSafe list).

lib/knowledge/jobs.ts, lib/knowledge/sharp-ingestion-job.ts, and scripts/run-covered-job.mjs are private-only: jobs.ts re-exports the private enrichment/jobs.ts registry; sharp-ingestion-job.ts imports sharp-odds-ingestion.ts and request-policy.ts (both private); run-covered-job.mjs is the CLI entrypoint (`pnpm cron:run`) that imports github-actions-pipeline.ts, scoring-service.ts, and live-board.ts directly.

app/api/inngest/route.ts, lib/inngest/index.ts, and lib/inngest/sharp.ts are private-only: the Inngest route serves lib/inngest/index.ts's exported functions, which re-export lib/inngest/sharp.ts, which imports the private sharp-ingestion-job.ts. lib/inngest/client.ts itself (a thin Inngest SDK wrapper) has no private import and was left unclassified/leaf-safe rather than excluded, since nothing about it is sensitive on its own.

lib/knowledge/enrichment/basketball.test.ts, lib/knowledge/sharp-rotation.test.ts, and lib/scoring/scoring-service.test.ts are private-only despite the sibling-test auto-exclusion rule not catching them automatically: each imports a private module directly for integration-style testing (enrichment/jobs.ts and sharp-matching.ts; sharp-ingestion-job.ts; and lib/knowledge/scoring-service.ts respectively) rather than only testing its own same-named module. lib/scoring/scoring-service.test.ts in particular is a pre-existing path oddity -- it tests lib/knowledge/scoring-service.ts from a different directory (lib/scoring/), so the same-directory sibling rule can't infer the pairing automatically.

The conditional list is intentionally empty: every module previously listed as conditional has been resolved to publicSafe or privateOnly in this pass. A module should only be added back to conditional if a genuinely new ambiguous dependency is discovered.

lib/knowledge/wnba-e2e.test.ts and lib/knowledge/mlb-e2e.test.ts (added in session 2, offline WNBA/MLB end-to-end readiness) are private-only: both import lib/knowledge/scoring-service.ts directly to exercise the real scoring/publishability gate end-to-end. lib/knowledge/enrichment/mlb-schedule-live-gate.test.ts and lib/knowledge/supabase-fixture-harness.ts (the shared generic Supabase REST mock they and wnba-e2e/mlb-e2e use) import no private-only module and are public-safe by the same import-closure check.

This manifest is not an exhaustive file listing for the public export; it records only the previously-ambiguous boundary files and their resolution. Generic leaf plumbing (lib/types, lib/db/provider-cache.ts, lib/providers/provider-adapter.ts, lib/providers/env.ts, lib/providers/sport-season.ts, lib/providers/normalize-sharpapi.ts, lib/providers/provider-pull-audit.ts, lib/providers/player-avatar.ts, components/providers/provider-pull-browser.tsx) was manually verified during this pass to have no private-only imports and does not need a manifest entry for the checker to be accurate.

.env.example is private-only in the public export despite containing no real values (confirmed safe by the original migration audit): it lists variable names for providers and jobs that correspond only to excluded private code (e.g. OPENROUTER_API_KEY, SPORTSDATAIO_API_KEY, ODDSPAPI_API_KEY), which would be confusing or misleading in a public checkout with no corresponding code. The public export instead ships .env.public.example, a curated template scoped to variables actually referenced by included files, verified by grepping process.env usage across the exported tree. This is unrelated to scripts/public-export.mjs's belt-and-suspenders .env(\..*)? exclusion pattern, which now carves out any tracked file ending in exactly ".example" (case-insensitive) as a known-safe template convention -- .env.public.example and .dev.vars.example both rely on that carve-out to be included; this manifest entry is what keeps the private .env.example excluded despite it.

render.yaml is private-only in the public export: it describes a Render-based cron-service hosting architecture that AGENTS.md and docs/AGENT_HANDOFF.md no longer document as current (GitHub Actions now owns live orchestration; Cloudflare/OpenNext is the current application-hosting target; Vercel is dormant; Render is not mentioned as active). Its startCommands also invoke `pnpm run cron:run`, which depends on scripts/run-covered-job.mjs (already private-only) and would not function from this export regardless. Retained in the private repository for historical/operational reference since it may still describe a live fallback there -- this is a public-export-only exclusion, not a claim that the file is obsolete in the private repository.

.github/workflows/covered-live-pipeline.yml, mlb-targeted-repair.yml, wnba-data-ingestion.yml, and mlb-enrichment-backfill.yml are private-only in the public export: each invokes `pnpm run cron:run`, which depends on scripts/run-covered-job.mjs (private-only) and the now-removed `cron:run` package.json script (see scripts/public-export.mjs's planPackageJsonForExport). Triggering any of them from a public checkout would fail immediately with a missing-script error, not run anything meaningful. validate-targeted-repair.yml is private-only for the same class of reason: its one run: step directly names lib/ops/github-actions-pipeline.test.ts, which is private-only (paired with the excluded github-actions-pipeline.ts), so the command would error on a missing file if run as specified. deploy-cloudflare.yml, diagnostic-mlb-providers.yml, and diagnostic-wnba-scoreboard.yml remain public-safe: verified each of their run: steps and referenced scripts have zero private-only dependencies (the two diagnostic .mjs scripts import only Node built-ins).
